scenario_name: Shadow copy deletion and mass rename
executive_summary: Scenario 'Shadow copy deletion and mass rename' is classified as likely ransomware impact activity with critical severity at confidence 0.970. Observed 1 suspicious parent-child process chain event(s). ATT&CK coverage includes T1059, T1059.001, T1204, T1486, T1490.
key_evidence: Observed 1 suspicious parent-child process chain event(s).
Detected 1 recovery or shadow-copy deletion attempt(s).
File activity reached 3 total event(s), including 2 rename event(s).
Detected 2 file extension change event(s), consistent with ransomware impact behavior.
Triggered 3 detection rule(s), highest rule severity: critical.
Mapped to ATT&CK technique(s): T1059, T1059.001, T1204, T1486, T1490.

triggered_rules.title: Suspicious Shadow Copy Deletion
triggered_rules.description: Detects commands commonly used to delete Windows shadow copies before ransomware encryption.

triggered_rules.title: Suspicious Command Execution
triggered_rules.description: Detects high-risk scripting or download command patterns associated with initial execution and staging.

triggered_rules.title: Suspicious Parent-Child Process Chain
triggered_rules.description: Detects office or user-facing applications launching command interpreters or scripting engines often used in ransomware delivery.

mitre_attack_mapping.rationale: Mapped directly from triggered rule tag attack.t1059.
Mapped directly from triggered rule tag attack.t1059.001.
Mapped directly from triggered rule tag attack.t1204.
Inferred from ransomware-like file churn or extension changes consistent with encryption impact.
Mapped directly from triggered rule tag attack.t1490.

recommended_actions: Preserve volatile evidence and collect forensic artifacts.
Protect recovery infrastructure and verify shadow copy availability.
Review the suspicious process tree and terminate malicious processes if confirmed.
Assess impacted user files and block further write activity if possible.
analyst_notes: Objective: Simulate ransomware deleting recovery artifacts and renaming user files with a locked extension.
Most relevant ATT&CK techniques: T1059, T1059.001, T1204, T1486, T1490.
